summary: Matched many small item callback functions, mostly trivial two-argument wrappers that call it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), across a wide set of item source files. Also matched several small Ness PK Flash / PK Flash explode routines, corrected related prototypes, and renamed the Ness PK Fire Pillar header from itnessfirepillar.h to itnesspkfirepillar.h so includes align with the item source/module name.
what_changed: Converted numerous placeholder comments like `/// #it_80281144` into real functions of the form `void function(Item_GObj* gobj, Item_GObj* ref_gobj) { it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj); }`.
Added `#include "it/it_26B1.h"` to many item .c files so the matched wrapper callbacks can call `it_8026B894` with a declared prototype.
Matched `it_802AA7F0` in `itnesspkflush.c` as a bool-returning check of `GET_ITEM(gobj)->msid == 0x2`, and changed its header prototype from `enum_t` to `bool`.
Renamed the argument in `it_802AA7E4` from a generic `arg0` to `gobj` while preserving the behavior of returning `GET_ITEM(gobj)->owner`.
Matched `it_802AB3D0` in `itnesspkflush.c` as another `it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj)` wrapper.

Matched `it_802AFD2C` in `itnesspkflushexplode.c` as a void routine that fetches `Item* it = GET_ITEM(gobj)` and zeros velocity fields; the diff shows `it->x40_vel.z = it->x40_vel.y = it->x40_vel.z = 0;`.
Matched `it_802AFD6C` in `itnesspkflushexplode.c` as an `it_8026B894` wrapper and updated the header prototype for `it_802AFD2C` from unknown to `void it_802AFD2C(Item_GObj*)`.

Deleted `src/melee/it/items/itnessfirepillar.h` and created/populated `src/melee/it/items/itnesspkfirepillar.h` with the same visible declarations for `it_802AA75C`, `it_802AA7C4`, and `it_803F6B60` under a header guard matching the PK Fire Pillar file name.

Populated previously empty or near-empty files such as `itdrmariopill.c`, `itmariocape.c`, and `itnesspkfirepillar.c` with the correct local header include, needed item support include(s), and trivial matched callbacks.
Improved `itklap.h` prototypes by replacing several `UNK_RET` / `UNK_PARAMS` declarations with concrete `bool`, `void`, `Item_GObj*`, `Vec3*`, `s32`, and `f32` signatures visible in the diff.
smart_moves: Recognized a repeated decompilation pattern: many item callbacks at the end of item modules are one-line forwarding wrappers to `it_8026B894` with `(Item_GObj* gobj, Item_GObj* ref_gobj)`.
Applied the same matching tactic broadly across many item files, reducing placeholder count with low-risk, mechanically consistent code.

Used the real per-item header in files like `ithinoarashi.c`, `itklap.c`, `itmaril.c`, `itmsbomb.c`, `itmariocape.c`, and `itdrmariopill.c` instead of relying only on `<platform.h>` / `<placeholder.h>` or `it/forward.h`.
Kept header prototypes synchronized with matched C definitions, especially for `itnesspkflush.h`, `itnesspkflushexplode.h`, and `itklap.h`.
Corrected the Ness PK Fire Pillar header filename to reflect the actual item/module name, preventing future confusion with the non-PK `itnessfirepillar` name.
For simple boolean functions, used direct `bool` signatures and `return true` / `return false`-style semantics rather than leaving unknown return types.
decomp_lessons: When an unmatched item callback has signature-like usage `(Item_GObj*, Item_GObj*)`, check whether it is just a forwarding call to `it_8026B894`; this PR shows that pattern occurs across many unrelated item modules.
Trivial one-line wrappers are valuable matches because they eliminate placeholders and clarify item callback tables without needing deep behavioral reconstruction.
Adding the precise dependency header, here `it/it_26B1.h`, is the clean way to match calls to shared item helpers instead of relying on placeholders or implicit declarations.
After matching a function body, immediately update the corresponding header from `UNK_RET` / `UNK_PARAMS` to the real return and argument types so future callsites have type information.
Small item-specific helpers can often be matched from field access patterns: `GET_ITEM(gobj)->owner` gives a `Fighter_GObj*`, and checks of `Item::msid` can become bool-returning predicates.

Header file names should follow the source/module name. The rename from `itnessfirepillar.h` to `itnesspkfirepillar.h` is a reusable cleanup pattern when a historical placeholder name omits part of the item name.
When filling empty item source files, include the local item header first and then the minimal item helper headers needed for the matched body.
naming_conventions: Use `gobj` for the primary `Item_GObj*` parameter and `ref_gobj` for the secondary referenced item/object parameter in two-object item callbacks.
Use item module local headers named after the item source, e.g. `itklap.h`, `itmaril.h`, `itmariocape.h`, `itmsbomb.h`, `itnesspkfirepillar.h`.
Prefer `bool` return types for simple true/false item callbacks rather than leaving `UNK_RET` or using `enum_t` when evidence supports a boolean.
Header guards for newly populated item headers may use the pattern `_ITNESSPKFIREPILLAR_H_` as shown in `itnesspkfirepillar.h`.
Keep address comments in headers alongside prototypes, e.g. `/* 2AA7F0 */ bool it_802AA7F0(Item_GObj*);`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Match repeated forwarding callbacks as `void name(Item_GObj* gobj, Item_GObj* ref_gobj) { it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj); }`.
For functions returning an owner pointer, use `return GET_ITEM(gobj)->owner;` with a `Fighter_GObj*` return type.
For simple item-state predicates, a compare against `Item::msid` can be expressed as an explicit `if` returning `1` or `0`; in this PR `it_802AA7F0` returns true when `msid == 0x2`.
For routines touching item data fields, include `it/inlines.h` to access `GET_ITEM(gobj)` and use a local `Item* it` variable.

A large batch of previously unknown item functions can be advanced by scanning for identical call patterns to a shared helper and then adding only the helper include required for declaration visibility.
Prototype refinement in headers is part of the match: replace `UNK_RET name(UNK_PARAMS)` with concrete signatures once the body or call pattern is known.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body states the intent directly: several small functions in `itnesspkflush` and `itnesspkflushexplode`, plus matching calls to `it_8026B894` across item files where they were effectively one-liners.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining item callbacks with signature `void (*)(Item_GObj*, Item_GObj*)` that may also be simple wrappers around `it_8026B894`.
Search for remaining includes of `itnessfirepillar.h` to ensure the PK Fire Pillar rename is complete.
Audit other item headers for `UNK_RET` / `UNK_PARAMS` declarations corresponding to already matched simple bool or void functions.
Check whether other empty item .c files can be populated with local header includes and one-line `it_8026B894` callbacks.

Review `itnesspkflushexplode.c` velocity zeroing in `it_802AFD2C` against assembly if more evidence is needed, because the diff excerpt shows repeated assignment to `x40_vel.z` and `x40_vel.y` but not `x40_vel.x`.
